figures.caption: Figure 1. A brief overview of our method. We propose to absorb historical contexts and move beyond simple context memorization, focusing on preserving the causal relationship between historical context and future generations rather than directly reconstructing historical tokens. Specifically, we fine-tune the context-less model to behave identically to the original model with full contexts. This is achieved through a self-supervised optimization that synchronizes the hidden states or output behaviors of the two models. This example shows the context absorption process, where the model absorbs 3 historical tokens by synchronization on 5 future tokens. Our method ensures that the absorbed context retains its semantic and causal influence on future deductions.
